source: Official Path of Exile wiki and official forum references

Definition.text: Sap is an Elemental Ailment associated with Lightning that causes affected targets to deal reduced Damage.

Purpose.text: Apply a target-side damage output penalty from Lightning-based Hit interaction.

Core Rules.text: Sap magnitude scales from Lightning Damage of the Hit and is capped at a maximum damage penalty.

Internal Rules.text: Sap is processed as a non-damaging elemental ailment state and does not apply direct damage over time.

Calculation Order.text: 1) qualifying Lightning Hit occurs 2) compute Sap magnitude vs Ailment Threshold 3) apply non-damaging ailment modifiers 4) clamp to maximum penalty 5) hold state for 4 seconds.

Priority Rules.text: Sap uses alternate elemental-ailment magnitude behavior with lower caps than legacy non-damaging ailments.

Preconditions.text: A target must receive a Hit that can inflict elemental non-damaging ailments and pass Lightning ailment applicability checks.

Postconditions.text: Target damage output is reduced by Sap magnitude for the duration.

Limitations.text: Elemental ailment immunity/avoidance and effect-cap controls can reduce or block Sap.

Caps.text: Current documented maximum penalty is up to 20% reduced Damage.

Formula.text: Publicly collected sources confirm upper bound and scaling basis but do not provide a full canonical closed-form formula in this pass.

requires: target not immune to elemental ailments
prevents: unbounded target damage output from one source
cannot_affect: Damage over Time direct damage application

Conflicts.text: Sap is distinct from Shock and does not map to Action Speed or Freeze/Chill style movement-state rules.

Exceptions.text: Forum and community captures show historical display wording variation (2s/4s), with current stable form represented as 4s in collected references.

Hidden Rules.text: Sap applies to targets already tracking non-damaging elemental ailment channels and is maintained as a separate state per ailment type.

Developer Notes.text: Model Sap as alternate non-damaging Lightning ailment with explicit version tagging and separate effect caps.

Historical Changes.text: Sap belongs to alternate ailment branch introduced in 3.5; later patch notes and community references describe lower caps than traditional Shock/Chill models.

text.note: Sap introduced as alternate ailment for Lightning.
Alternate elemental ailments (including Sap) documented as lower-cap models.

text: High Lightning Hit can apply higher Sap up to ~20% reduced outgoing damage at max magnitude.
Sap is not a damage-over-time ailment; it is a non-damaging penalty effect.
Sap can coexist with other non-damaging elemental ailments in independent states.

requires: target not immune to elemental ailments
cannot_affect: non-ailment-based flat reductions not linked to this state
